subject: [PATCH v1] writeback: skip sync(2) inode writeback for filesystems with no data integrity guarantees

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that while sync(2) may not provide data integrity guarantees, it still flushed data for non-broken FUSE servers, and suggested kicking a flush worker to do writeback in the background for filesystems with SB_I_NO_DATA_INTEGRITY
reviews.raw_body:
I'd note that previously, if the FUSE server was not broken, although
sync(2) would not provide any data integrity guarantee, it would still
flush the data so practically, there would be no user observable difference
unless you really did powerfail testing. So some users might be
unpleasantly surprised by sync(2) suddently not doing anything on FUSE
filesystems. Maybe for SB_I_NO_DATA_INTEGRITY filesystems we should at
least kick flush worker to do writeback in the background?

								Honza

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges that the patch needs to skip sync(2) inode writeback for filesystems without data integrity guarantees, agrees to add a check in v2 and promises to make the change.
reviews.raw_body:
That's a great point. I'll make this change for v2.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-03-19.patch_summary: This patch adds a new superblock flag, SB_I_NO_DATA_INTEGRITY, to identify filesystems that cannot guarantee data persistence on sync operations. It then uses this flag to skip the inode writeback for these filesystems during sync(2) operations, improving efficiency and preventing potential hangs. The change replaces a per-inode flag with a superblock-level flag, as data integrity is a filesystem-wide property. This patch restores fuse's previous behavior before tmp folios were removed, where sync was essentially a no-op.
